channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Kennedy Center appeals judge's ruling to remove Trump's name
item.description: Friday is a deadline for the Kennedy Center to remove President Trump's name from all of its branding, including the marble front of its building in Washington, D.C.
item.content:encoded: Friday is a deadline for the Kennedy Center to remove President Trump's name from all of its branding, including the marble front of its building in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Blair

item.title: Trump cancels further strikes on Iran, announcing peace deal is imminent
item.description: President Trump said Thursday that he was canceling strikes on Iran as talks were back on with Iran and a peace deal was imminent.
item.content:encoded: President Trump said Thursday that he was canceling strikes on Iran as talks were back on with Iran and a peace deal was imminent.
item.dc:creator: Aya Batrawy

item.title: David Hockney, one of the most influential artists of the 20th century, dies at 88
item.description: Hockney moved from London to Southern California in the 1960s and was an innovative painter, photographer, stage designer and printmaker.
item.content:encoded: Hockney moved from London to Southern California in the 1960s and was an innovative painter, photographer, stage designer and printmaker.
item.dc:creator: Susan Stamberg

item.title: Former State Department advisor on the peace talks between the U.S. and Iran
item.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks former State Department advisor and negotiator Aaron David Miller about peace talks and Iran's strategic options following President Trump's announcement that a deal is close.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Leila Fadel asks former State Department advisor and negotiator Aaron David Miller about peace talks and Iran's strategic options following President Trump's announcement that a deal is close.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Pope Leo defends migrants at 'dock of shame' in Spain
item.description: Pope Leo XIV visited the Canary Islands on Thursday, where he issued a forceful defense of migrants.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV visited the Canary Islands on Thursday, where he issued a forceful defense of migrants.

(Image credit: Alessandra Tarantino)
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: She waited decades for Scotland to make the World Cup. At 93, she'll cheer in person
item.description: Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13. "I'm the luckiest person in this world," she says.
item.content:encoded:
Moira Brown, perhaps the oldest of Scotland's Tartan Army of soccer fans, will be in Boston when Scotland's team plays against Haiti on June 13. "I'm the luckiest person in this world," she says.

(Image credit: Lauren Frayer)
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: The U.S. dazzles in its World Cup opener, dominating Paraguay 4-1
item.description: Striker Folarin Balogun knocked in two goals, the second a one-on-two stunner to the upper corner of the net to end the first half. For the Americans, it was an impressive start to a home World Cup.
item.content:encoded:
Striker Folarin Balogun knocked in two goals, the second a one-on-two stunner to the upper corner of the net to end the first half. For the Americans, it was an impressive start to a home World Cup.

(Image credit: Dean Mouhtaropoulos)
item.dc:creator: Becky Sullivan

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump says a deal with Iran will be announced "soon," White House readies for UFC event as Trump navigates rocky political ground, Trump names new nominee for national intelligence director.
item.content:encoded: Trump says a deal with Iran will be announced "soon," White House readies for UFC event as Trump navigates rocky political ground, Trump names new nominee for national intelligence director.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Sublime's Jakob Nowell 'never wanted any part of' replacing his father
item.description: Thirty years ago, Brad Nowell, the lead singer of '90s ska band Sublime, died of an overdose. His son Jakob was an infant. He's now Sublime's lead singer, releasing the band's first album since 1996.
item.content:encoded:
Thirty years ago, Brad Nowell, the lead singer of '90s ska band Sublime, died of an overdose. His son Jakob was an infant. He's now Sublime's lead singer, releasing the band's first album since 1996.

(Image credit: Micala Austin)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: New study finds 'foundation' species continue to shape ecosystems after their death
item.description: Foundation species like coral, oysters and big trees are critical to their ecosystems, providing food and shelter. A new study finds their influence continues after their death.
item.content:encoded: Foundation species like coral, oysters and big trees are critical to their ecosystems, providing food and shelter. A new study finds their influence continues after their death.
item.dc:creator: Nathan Rott
